description.content:
Resolves or initializes the component-group shard used by the portal shell.
Observed as a body-less POST returning a boolean.

Returns counts for multiple OneDrive Sync report filters in a single request.
The request body is an array of filter expressions, and the response is keyed
by filter index.
Returns OneDrive Sync client version distribution grouped by update ring and platform type.
Returns the tenant eligibility record that gates M365 Apps admin center features.

Returns agreement records that gate preview or ring-based onboarding experiences.
The observed live request filtered the collection with `$filter=FeatureRing eq 'Preview'` and returned an empty `value` array for the current tenant.
OData filter used to scope onboarding, agreement, or feature provisioning records.
OData filter used to scope onboarding, agreement, or feature provisioning records.
Returns feature metadata, ring assignments, and eligibility requirements used by the portal.
Returns provisioning state for one or more features, typically scoped with an OData filter.
OData filter used to scope feature provisioning records.
OData filter used to scope onboarding, agreement, or feature provisioning records.

item.name: Provision or confirm a feature state

request.name: Provision or confirm a feature state

description.content:
Observed as a body-less POST that returns an OData boolean wrapper.
Treat this as a potentially state-changing provisioning call.
Returns the release catalog used by Device Configuration and release-selection experiences.

The M365 Apps Services API is used by the Microsoft 365 Apps admin center
to query onboarding state, feature availability, release catalogs, component
sharding, and OneDrive Sync health telemetry from `clients.config.office.net`.

This surface was observed while browsing:

  - Home and Cloud Update
  - Device Configuration
  - OneDrive Sync
  - Setup

Requests are issued from the portal with bearer tokens and diagnostic headers
such as `x-api-name`, `x-correlationid`, `x-manageoffice-client-sid`, and
`x-requested-with`.

Static bundle analysis also exposed related or preview hosts including
`canary.clients.config.office.net` and `sip.clients.config.office.net`,
but the operations below were the concrete requests observed in the current
tenant.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best-efforts basis and
may not be 100% accurate.
